reviews.author: Sinja Haag
reviews.text: A nice spot with a very cute water- and fire show at night. Not overly exciting but definitely worth a visit. No need to book a boat, you can sit along the river and enjoy nice street food and some drinks whilst watching the scenery starting at 9pm on weekends!

reviews.author: Kedar Kalkote
reviews.text: Honestly, I wouldn't call this a 'must-visit.' While the idea of a fire-breathing bridge is cool. If you don't like being pushed around in a sea of people for a very short show, you’re better off skipping the crowd and just seeing the bridge when it's lit up on a quiet weekday evening.

reviews.author: SuMit Chauhan
reviews.text: Iconic landmark of Da Nang and a must-visit spot. The bridge looks stunning both during the day and at night with the lights on. The fire and water show on weekends is the main attraction and worth watching. Great views of the river, lively atmosphere, and perfect for photos and evening walks. Definitely one of the highlights of the city. 🐉✨

reviews.author: Nails and Art by Gıe
reviews.text: One of the best experience you must see. Mixed of excitement and surprises. While everybody was in awe watching the dragon fire we were surprised with a very quick shower of very cold water that no one had ever scape.

reviews.author: Z M
reviews.text: Dragon bridge cool. They have the show at 9 pm Friday, Saturday and Sunday. Play basically close down the bridge for 10 minutes and the dragon shoots fire and water. People will gather on the bridge 30 minutes before but you can see it from both side of the streets as well. It's just different something I haven't seen in other places. Again its cool to see it once.

reviews.author: Christoper James Sumpit
reviews.text: One of the icons of Da Nang. It has a fire and water show every weekends on 9pm. I wouldn't say that it's a must see, don't fret yourself if you're only at Da Nang during the weekdays, the city has other beautiful places to see and go to. But if you're at the city during the weekends then it wouldn't hurt to see the show. The show looks more better from a distance, because if you're close then you wouldn't see the whole bridge. If you're going to watch it up close I suggest you bring an umbrella since you'll be within the spray area of the water. You can wait until the show starts at the nearby Son Tra market. Have dinner or buy merchandise there, then make your way towards the bridge 5 to 7 minutes before the show starts so you can secure a spot.

reviews.author: Moonlight85
reviews.text: Dragon Bridge in Da Nang, Vietnam, illuminated at night over the Han River. The bridge is a famous landmark known for its unique design shaped like a golden dragon, symbolizing power and prosperity in Vietnamese culture. It is about 650 meters long and was opened in 2013. On weekend nights (Friday, Saturday, and Sunday) at 9:00 PM, the dragon's head performs a spectacular show, breathing fire and then water into the air. The bridge's colorful LED lighting, as seen in the image, reflects beautifully on the river, attracting crowds along the riverbanks and on boat cruises.

reviews.author: Stabile Simone
reviews.text: The Dragon Bridge is absolutely beautiful, especially the show in the evening. It’s truly impressive to watch. The only recommendation I would give is this: after the fire show, there is also a water show. Make sure to check which direction the wind is blowing and avoid standing directly under the dragon — otherwise, you will end up completely soaked.

reviews.author: Priyal Shah
reviews.text: This spot is great for a visit! 🤩 I loved soaking in the local atmosphere. The show, which is about 30 minutes, runs only on weekends, Friday to Sunday. ✨🌉 Perfect for a quick, enjoyable experience! 😊

reviews.author: Alicia Brant
reviews.text: The Dragon Bridge is simply amazing. We took in views from the people bridge and along the river walk near Euro Village. It is a well landscaped area with beautiful plants. We are going back on the weekend to see the Fire and Water show.

reviews.author: Jeff Crawford
reviews.text: The Dragon Bridge is worth seeing. I suggest going to restaurant like The View for a great spot to watch from. There are many restaurants and bars close with outside seating. The show is at 9PM on the weekends. You will also see the prettiest view of the Da Nang skyline from the side of the bridge where the dragon's head is. At 9PM the Dragon breathes fire several times and then sprays water several times. Surprisingly, the water actually looks more cool than the fire!

reviews.author: Tasha (AutismFamilyTravelGuide)
reviews.text: Dragon Bridge — Cầu Rồng — is one of Đà Nẵng’s most iconic landmarks, and it’s impossible to miss its bright golden dragon stretching proudly across the river. By day, it’s a striking piece of architecture, but by night it becomes a real spectacle, glowing with colourful lights that reflect beautifully on the water. If you’re in Đà Nẵng on a weekend, the highlight is the dragon’s fire and water show, usually on Saturday and Sunday evenings. Crowds gather to watch the dragon breathe real flames and spray water — a fun, lively experience for families, couples, and anyone seeing the city for the first time. The bridge is perfectly placed for a riverside walk, with easy access to cafés, night markets, and viewpoints along both sides of the river. Whether you cross it on foot, drive over it, or admire it from below, it’s a must-see symbol of Đà Nẵng’s modern, energetic spirit.

reviews.author: Richard Magpantay
reviews.text: It’s cool to see the dragon breathe fire and water, but manage your expectations, don’t expect a mind-blowing show. It’s a fun experience if you’re already in the area, but I wouldn’t say it’s a huge “wow” moment. Still worth checking! Loved walking here.

reviews.author: J S
reviews.text: Dragon Bridge Danang is an amazing place to visit both during day and night time. The walk along the river is awesome. There’s Fire and Water show at 9PM on weekends-Friday, Saturday and Sunday. Better check online for latest info. Take a jacket or watch from far to escape from water during the show. It’s get really crowded, so go early for a good spot and also to visit the places near the river. After the show, wait or walk little bit to grab a taxi. There’s a good Indian restaurant opposite the road.

reviews.author: Les Jaco
reviews.text: The Dragon Bridge in Da Nang is truly iconic! I was lucky to stay nearby, so I got to see it up close both in the morning and at night. The bridge is an incredible sight during the day, but at night it really comes alive. If you visit on Friday, Saturday, or Sunday at 9 PM, you can catch the amazing fire and water show, the dragon literally breathes fire, and it’s an unforgettable experience. Below the bridge, there are food stalls where you can grab local snacks while enjoying the view.

reviews.author: vinh lam
reviews.text: Such an iconic and beautiful bridge. It’s very pretty to walk over and there were lots of food/ drink vendors under it. The vantage point to see the fire/water show is amazing underneath the bridge. Wouldn’t recommend standing on the bridge next to it since the flame is quite hot and the water show will absolutely leave you drenched. Better to sit a little way out and enjoy 😊

reviews.author: Hemanth Shriyan
reviews.text: This Bridge and the surrounding areas have a special vibe. Evening times are pleasant. You have the fire and water show conducted on every Friday, Saturday and Sunday from 9 pm till 9.15 pm. The whole bridge is lit and the show is awesome for kids and Adults alike. Be there on the right side of the bridge by 7.30 Pm and reserve the best Riverside seat to watch the show.

reviews.author: Annie Thomas
reviews.text: It was a delight to finally see the Dragon bridge in Da Nang! An architectural marvel! A must visit spot if you dre in Da Nang! It's beautiful as nd stands out beautifully lit at night with changing colours and extending in magnificence during the day! You cannot leave the city without visiting this marvel! Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Karim Azzam
reviews.text: Very nice bridge 🌉 they make the show for the dragon on Friday, Saturday and Sunday at 9:00 pm. The show stay until 9:20 pm. Be early there to have good place for filming. There is a night market under the bridge very nice ❤️❤️❤️

reviews.author: ko.けおー卵
reviews.text: I visited Dragon Bridge in Da Nang and it was one of the most impressive landmarks in the city. The massive golden dragon design is truly iconic and looks even more stunning in person. Walking across the bridge gave us amazing views of the Han River and the surrounding skyline. The structure is beautifully designed and very clean, making it pleasant to explore both during the day and at night. I especially recommend visiting in the evening when the bridge lights up—it creates a fantastic atmosphere and is perfect for photos. The dragon’s detailed design is incredible and makes this bridge unique compared to any other bridge I have seen. It is easy to access and a must-visit spot for anyone traveling to Da Nang. Whether you are taking a walk, riding a bike, or just stopping for pictures, Dragon Bridge is definitely worth seeing. I highly recommend adding it to your travel list.

reviews.author: noel medina
reviews.text: Dragon Bridge in Da Nang is an iconic landmark and an easy stop along the Han River, best seen in the evening. On Friday, Saturday, and Sunday around 9:00 pm, the bridge becomes especially popular, with lighting and activity drawing more people to the area. After dark, the lights reflect on the water and highlight the dragon design. A light river breeze makes it more comfortable, and while traffic noise is present, it blends into the surrounding city atmosphere. Crowds gather at night but remain manageable. There is no ticket or line, making it a natural stop during an evening walk rather than a place that requires advance planning.

reviews.author: Tony Chan
reviews.text: Free of charge attraction that's a bucket list for Da Nang. The fire breathing and water spout only happens on Friday, Saturday and Sunday. Both sides of the traffic will be stopped during the performance.

reviews.author: Angelica Bosch
reviews.text: Not entirely sure what all the fuss is about. Hundreds of adults gather crowding the place to see a dragon head spitting some fire and lots of water. Excessively touristy and naive for my taste. The so-called "show" takes place 9pm on weekends and lasts about 15 minutes. The Dragon Bridge ( Cầu Rồng) itself is shaped like a giant golden dragon crossing the Han River, symbolizing power and prosperity in Vietnamese culture. It opened in 2013 and is about 666 meters long with 6 lanes.

reviews.author: Tim Ward
reviews.text: I have been coming to Danang, VN since 2009. I remember when they were just starting to build the dragon bridge around 2011, it is wonderful and every Friday/Saturday night at 9pm, it blows fire from the mouthpiece. Very charming surrounded by the most beautiful parts of the City

reviews.author: jervin joseph
reviews.text: Spectacular show, we enjoyed it a lot, the show is only on the weekends (Friday,Saturday and Sunday) which begins at 9 pm and end by 9:10 pm. Make sure to reach early and get a perfect place to watch the show, it gets crowded. If u are on the bridge near the head of the dragon would suggest u to carry umbrella or raincoat as the water show will surely drench u fully.

reviews.author: Antoni Mysona
reviews.text: The Dragon Bridge is an easy and tourist-friendly stop, with a night market right next to it. The fire-breathing dragon show runs every Friday, Saturday, and Sunday at 9:00 pm and can be viewed from both sides of the bridge and from the bridge itself. Around Christmas, expect limited viewing spots. Street vendors often set up tables and chairs limited for their own customers, and a large Christmas tree and decorations can block some of the best views. Finding a clear spot can be tricky unless you squeeze in a photo opportunity in between trees or watch from a nearby café. The dragon only breathes fire a few times before switching to water, which was a bit disappointing, and the whole show lasts less than 5 minutes. On our visit, the bridge was closed to traffic about 15 minutes beforehand, allowing people to walk onto the road and take photos. Security was visible, and everything felt safe and well organized. Worth seeing once—just don’t expect a long or dramatic show.

reviews.author: Blankita Styles
reviews.text: Im actually on the bridge right now. I’ve crossed it may times durning my stay and at night time it really lights up. It’s super safe to be here and you have a very cute night market at the end of the bride on the head side. Been a few times and it’s always magical

reviews.author: Russ Lacuata
reviews.text: Its definitely an attraction. The main event is the Fire and Water show every Friday, Saturday and Sunday at 9p. The dragon shoots fire and water at the crowd and last like 10-15 minutes. It’s a crowd pleaser for tourists and locals. I do suggest seeing it up close during the day and also at night. It is an architectural masterpiece.

reviews.author: Sophia Nicole Bernales
reviews.text: It was a pleasant experience to have for a short time before heading to the night market to shop. Would have been more awesome if there was music while the fire and water show was on going.

reviews.author: Yuk Ming Fong
reviews.text: The dragon bridge is one of the most iconic structures in Da Nang. There is a fire show but we did not have time to see it. We were there around 20:30-21:30 on 25-Dec-2025 but, sadly, it did not have the fire show.
